description:
The diagram illustrates the architecture of the CV Assistant single-page application (SPA). The overall layout is a vertically stacked arrangement, emphasizing the hierarchical structure of the navigation groups and their views. The background is a very light sky blue.

At the top is a rounded rectangle labeled "CV Assistant SPA (localhost:8420)". It has a slightly thicker, medium gray border. Below this, a larger, rounded-corner rectangle represents the main UI, divided into two sections: a left-hand collapsible sidebar and a larger right-hand content area.

The sidebar is a narrower column on the left, filled with a slightly darker shade of sky blue than the background. Within the sidebar, six distinct groups are stacked vertically, each represented by a rounded-corner rectangle with a soft white fill and a thin, dashed medium-gray border. Each group has a title in bold, sans-serif white text.

1.  **CHAT**: Contains a single view, represented by a smaller rectangle labeled "Main Chat" in sans-serif text. A small, friendly chat bubble icon is to the left of the label.
2.  **AGENTS**: Contains three views: "All Agents" (represented by a grid icon), "Agent Chat", and "Pinned skills". The "Pinned skills" view is indicated by a pin icon.
3.  **CONTROL**: Contains five views: "Overview" (represented by a dashboard icon), "Channels" (represented by a connection icon), "Models" (represented by a chip icon), "Sessions" (represented by a people icon), and "Jobs" (represented by a calendar icon).
4.  **CORE**: Contains three views: "Skills" (represented by a wrench icon), "Datasets" (represented by a database icon), and "Powers" (represented by a key icon).
5.  **RESEARCH**: Contains four views: "Knowledge Vault" (represented by a folder icon), "Knowledge Graph" (represented by a graph icon), "Specs" (represented by a document icon), and "Digests" (represented by a list icon).
6.  **SETTINGS**: Contains four views: "Config" (represented by a gear icon), "Cache" (represented by a trash can icon), "Debug" (represented by a bug icon), and "Logs" (represented by a terminal icon).

Each view in the sidebar is connected to its respective group with a thin, dashed medium-gray line.

The content area to the right of the sidebar is a large, white rectangle. Inside the content area, a placeholder text "Content Area" is written in light gray sans-serif font. A soft-gray arrow with a rounded tip points from each view in the sidebar towards the "Content Area", indicating that selecting a view will display its content in this area.

Below the entire diagram, a small text annotation reads: "Collapsible Sidebar: 6 Navigation Groups, 17 Views" in a muted gray sans-serif font.

critique.revised_description:
The diagram illustrates the architecture of the CV Assistant single-page application (SPA). The overall layout is a vertically stacked arrangement, emphasizing the hierarchical structure of the navigation groups and their views. The background is a very light sky blue.

At the top is a rounded rectangle labeled "CV Assistant SPA (localhost:8420)". It has a slightly thicker, medium gray border. Below this, a larger, rounded-corner rectangle represents the main UI, divided into two sections: a left-hand collapsible sidebar and a larger right-hand content area.

The sidebar is a narrower column on the left, filled with a slightly darker shade of sky blue than the background. Within the sidebar, six distinct groups are stacked vertically, each represented by a rounded-corner rectangle with a soft white fill and a thin, dashed medium-gray border. Each group has a title in bold, sans-serif white text.

1.  **CHAT**: Contains a single view, represented by a smaller rectangle labeled "Main Chat" in sans-serif text. A small, friendly chat bubble icon is to the left of the label.
2.  **AGENTS**: Contains three views: "All Agents" (represented by a grid icon), "Agent Chat" (represented by a list icon), and "Pinned skills" (indicated by a pin icon).
3.  **CONTROL**: Contains five views: "Overview" (represented by a dashboard icon), "Channels" (represented by a connection icon), "Models" (represented by a chip icon), "Sessions" (represented by a people icon), and "Jobs" (represented by a calendar icon).
4.  **CORE**: Contains three views: "Skills" (represented by a wrench icon), "Datasets" (represented by a database icon), and "Powers" (represented by a key icon).
5.  **RESEARCH**: Contains four views: "Knowledge Vault" (represented by a folder icon), "Knowledge Graph" (represented by a graph icon), "Specs" (represented by a document icon), and "Digests" (represented by a list icon).
6.  **SETTINGS**: Contains four views: "Config" (represented by a gear icon), "Cache" (represented by a trash can icon), "Debug" (represented by a bug icon), and "Logs" (represented by a terminal icon).

Each view in the sidebar is connected to the "Content Area" with a soft-gray arrow with a rounded tip, indicating that selecting a view will display its content in this area.

The content area to the right of the sidebar is a large, white rectangle. Inside the content area, placeholder text "Content Area" is written in light gray sans-serif font.

Below the entire diagram, a small text annotation reads: "Collapsible Sidebar: 6 Navigation Groups, 17 Views" in a muted gray sans-serif font.
